prevalence.value: ~0.3-1% (workforce); ~10-15% of adult asthma attributable to work exposures
prevalence.source: European occupational health surveillance; occupational asthma epidemiology syntheses
prevalence.trend: stable; prevention reduces incidence in regulated workplaces

burden.dalyPerCapita: low overall; significant productivity loss and chronic asthma burden for affected workers

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Work-related symptom pattern with objective confirmation (spirometry/peak flow variation); specialist evaluation to identify sensitizers and guide workplace controls

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: workplace surveillance varies; occupational health services common in many regulated sectors
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Hundreds of thousands (subset of adult asthma across EU)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with exposure control and guideline-based asthma therapy

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Occupational medicine and pulmonology pathways facilitate confirmation and workplace interventions
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Exposure control/removal, workplace adjustments, PPE, guideline-based ICS therapy, documentation for occupational disease reporting/compensation
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Ensuring consistent reporting and coverage in small businesses and contractors
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong occupational health regulation and access to diagnostics and evidence-based asthma therapy
